content.problemStatement:
Chef and his friend is standing on the X-axis at the points X1X_1X1​ and X2X_2X2​ respectively.

Chef moves one step forward each second (that is he moves to X1+1X_1 + 1X1​+1 after the 1st1^{st}1st second, X1+2X_1 + 2X1​+2 after the 2nd2^{nd}2nd second, and so on), whereas his friend moves 222 steps forward each second (that is he moves to X2+2X_2 + 2X2​+2 after the 1st1^{st}1st second, X2+4X_2 + 4X2​+4 after the 2nd2^{nd}2nd second, and so on).

You need to determine if Chef will be able to meet his friend or not. You can assume that Chef and his friend both keep on moving for a long indefinite amount of time and also that they move **simultaneously**.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contain two space-separated integers X1X_1X1​ and X2X_2X2​ - the position of Chef and his friend at the start respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line `YES` (without quotes) if Chef can meet his friend, and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YeS`, `YEs`, `yes` and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X1 and X2 — the initial positions of Chef and his friend on the X‑axis.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef can meet his friend at some moment (including time 0), otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: X1 == X2, they are already together.\nTest case 2: X1 < X2, Chef is slower and behind, so they never meet.\nTest case 3: X1 > X2, they meet after t = X1 - X2 = 6 seconds at position 13.
First case: 5 >= 3, so Chef catches up after 2 seconds.\nSecond case: 2 < 9, Chef can never meet his friend.
Chef starts far ahead (1e9) and will meet his friend after 999999999 seconds.

solution.approach: At time t (t >= 0) Chef is at position X1 + t and his friend is at X2 + 2*t. They meet when X1 + t = X2 + 2*t, which simplifies to t = X1 - X2. A non‑negative integer t exists iff X1 >= X2. Therefore the answer is YES when X1 >= X2, otherwise NO. The algorithm processes each test case independently in O(1) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        X1 = int(next(it)); X2 = int(next(it))
        out.append('YES' if X1 >= X2 else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
